commit.message:
Clean up glob path expansion (#168)

The old `expand_path_pattern` used a fragile sequential prefix-swap list
to reverse-transform glob results back to user-facing paths, and had no
environment variable expansion support.

### Changes

- **Split at last `/`** — separates the pattern into `raw_prefix`
(preserved verbatim for output) and `rhs_pattern` (the glob portion)
- **`expanded_prefix` pipeline** — builds the absolute path used for
globbing by delegating to bash's own filename expansion logic
- **`bash_funcs::expand_filename`** — new function that calls
`bash_expand_filename` from `bashline.c` directly via a symbol declared
in `bash_symbols.rs`; returns the original filename unchanged if
expansion fails
- **New `bash_symbols` declaration** — `bash_expand_filename`
- **Result mapping** — strips `expanded_prefix` from each glob result to
get the relative portion, then prepends `raw_prefix` to preserve the
user's original spelling in completions
- **`PathPatternExpansion` struct** — replaces the `(String,
Vec<(String, String)>)` return type with named fields

### Example

```
pattern: "$HOME/projects/fly*"
  raw_prefix:      "$HOME/projects"
  expanded_prefix: "/home/user/projects"   (after bash filename expansion)
  rhs_pattern:     "fly*"
  glob runs:       "/home/user/projects/fly*"
  result path:     "/home/user/projects/flyline"
  suggestion:      "$HOME/projects/flyline"   ← raw_prefix preserved
```

docs: rewrite Balancing track B2–B8 with worktree-isolated iterative approach (#43)

Replace the old B2–B6 (monolithic runner/analysis pipeline) with a new
B2–B8 structure centered on per-discipline worktree isolation:

- B2: General config bypass — manual quick-win optimizations
- B3: Per-discipline runners in isolated worktrees/branches
- B4: Per-discipline analysis with mutation proposals
- B5: Mutation validation runners in separate worktrees
- B6: Manual iteration loop (alternate B4↔B5)
- B7: (Future) Whole-game cross-discipline optimization
- B8: (Future) Automated iteration loop

Key design: each discipline runner and each mutation test gets its own
git worktree on a dedicated branch, enabling parallel execution and
safe config modification. Results are committed to branches for audit.

Also adds vision/roadmap improvement suggestions file.

commit.message:
fix: tpool+tempfile for curl — no pipes, no poll, no deadlock

Previous approach used Popen.poll() which is broken under eventlet
(patches os.waitpid). New approach: subprocess.run() with no PIPE
(curl writes to temp file via -o flag), executed inside
eventlet.tpool.execute() where real os.waitpid works correctly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use temp file instead of pipes for curl output

eventlet patches os.read/write which corrupts Popen PIPE handling after
the first call. Now curl writes to a temp file (-o flag) which we read
back with plain open(). Also properly reap zombie processes with wait().

Verified in Docker: poll loop runs 7+ iterations in 30s without hanging.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

```rust
// After

```

successful call. Switch to Popen with a poll() loop that calls

eventlet event loop while curl runs in a separate process.

network stack. Previous curl timeouts were caused by missing

fix: use real (un-patched) sockets for Telegram HTTP

eventlet patches socket/ssl modules, making all Python HTTP libraries
unreliable in green threads. Use eventlet.patcher.original() to get
the real socket/ssl modules and build raw HTTPS connections that bypass
all monkey-patching. Combined with tpool for non-blocking execution.

commit.message:
feat(#9): Bootstrap Electron + React app skeleton with build pipeline (#65)

## What does this PR do?

Bootstraps the \onesell-client\ Electron desktop application with a
React renderer, Vite build pipeline, hot-reload dev mode, and production
build configuration via electron-builder. This is the foundation all
subsequent UI and extraction work (issues #10#25) depends on.

## Linked Issue
Closes #9

## Approach

The client uses a **dual-tsconfig** pattern to handle the split between
the Electron main process (CommonJS, Node.js APIs) and the React
renderer (ESM, DOM APIs, bundled by Vite):

- \ sconfig.main.json\ compiles \src/main/**\ to \dist/main/\ as
CommonJS. This is what Electron's \main\ entry point loads.
- \ sconfig.json\ type-checks \src/renderer/**\ and \src/shared/**\ with
DOM libs; \
oEmit: true\ because Vite owns the renderer build.
- \ ite.config.ts\ builds renderer to \dist/renderer/\, sets \ ase:
'./'\ so the built HTML works with Electron's \ ile://\ protocol.

Security baseline (P1 / P9) is applied from day one:
- \contextIsolation: true\, \
odeIntegration: false\, \sandbox: true\ in BrowserWindow webPreferences.
- \preload.ts\ exposes an empty \lectronAPI\ object via
\contextBridge\; renderer has zero direct Node.js access.

Hot-reload in dev mode: \pnpm electron:dev\ runs \ ite dev\ and
\lectron .\ concurrently; the main process loads
\http://localhost:5173\, giving full Vite HMR.

## Tests added

- \ ests/unit/app.test.tsx\ 2 unit tests verifying the placeholder App
renders the heading and tagline (jsdom environment via vitest).

All pass: \pnpm test:unit\  2/2 

## Developer checklist
- [x] All acceptance criteria implemented and manually verified
- [x] No architectural principles violated (P1/P9 enforced in main
process config)
- [x] No credentials, keys, or secrets in this diff
- [x] No \ny\ types without justification
- [x] \pnpm test:unit\ passing (2/2); \pnpm build\ passing; \pnpm
typecheck\ passing

---------

commit.message:
fix: defer background tasks to first HTTP request + HTTP startup probe

Background greenlets spawned at module-import time never get scheduled
because eventlet's hub isn't running yet. TCP startup probes don't
trigger the event loop either.

Solution: use @app.before_request to start all background tasks
(Telegram, dream, healer, PR monitor, Moltbook) on the first HTTP
request. Also switch Cloud Run to HTTP startup probe (/health) so
the first request happens immediately on deploy.

urllib.request.urlopen still uses the eventlet-patched socket layer
even with explicit SSL context, causing UNEXPECTED_EOF_WHILE_READING.
http.client.HTTPSConnection with its own ssl context fully bypasses
the eventlet corruption. Tested: getMe returns 200 immediately.

fix: add explicit SSL context to _tg_http to prevent eventlet corruption

Eventlet monkey-patches the socket layer which corrupts SSL handshakes,
causing 'UNEXPECTED_EOF_WHILE_READING' on every Telegram API call.
Using ssl.create_default_context() bypasses the corrupted path.

Add automatic theme selection for macOS system appearance

Define tviti/theme in user-globals.el. Set tviti/theme in macos-config.el using ns-system-appearance for Emacs 29+ to match system dark/light mode. Load the selected theme after initialization in init.el using an after-init-hook.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Milestone reuse discipline effects (#40)

* BREAKING: Milestones reuse discipline card effects with best values

Milestone encounters no longer define their own separate card effects.
Instead, they look up the discipline's existing EnemyCardEffects and use
roll_best_concrete_effect (max values) to create the most powerful
version of each effect.

Key changes:
- Add tier: u32 field to LibraryCard for tier-aware effect lookups
- Add roll_best_concrete_effect() that always picks max values
- Add Library methods: card_effects_for_discipline_and_tier,
  enemy_effects_for_discipline_and_tier, add_card_with_tier
- Refactor all 5 register_*_milestone functions to derive from
  the discipline's base encounter definition instead of hardcoding
- Combat milestones classify enemy effects into deck categories
- Gathering milestones (mining/herbalism/woodcutting/fishing) extract
  the base encounter structure and rebuild with best-rolled effects
- generate_milestone_reward_effects now also scales EnemyCardEffects
  and tags new effects with the next tier number

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add vision/roadmap suggestions for milestone effect reuse

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Preserve milestone deck composition on tier escalation

When a player beats a milestone and the next tier is generated, the new
milestone now mirrors the defeated milestone's deck structure instead of
rebuilding from scratch or from the base encounter.

Key changes:
- Add Library::replace_card() for in-place card replacement at same ID
- Generalize tier_effect_mapping() to accept from_tier/to_tier
- All 5 register_*_milestone functions accept optional previous def
- Combat milestone preserves per-deck card types and hand counts
- Non-combat milestones derive from defeated milestone (not base encounter)
- handle_milestone_finish extracts defeated inner encounter before replace
- Use scale_factor() consistently for tier scaling ratios
- Add 2 integration tests: deck composition + card ID preservation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
